libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Configure JVM test execution with `--add-opens=java.base/java.lang=ALL-UNNAMED` and `--add-opens=java.base/java.util=ALL-UNNAMED` when exercising OGNL reflective property/method access, map/list expressions, or expression compilation on JDK 17+. Do not set `ognl.security.manager` globally; if covering OGNL sandbox/security-manager paths, set and restore it inside the specific isolated test scenario, and make tests tolerate `System.setSecurityManager` being unsupported on newer JDKs.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #7430 [Automation] java run fails for ognl:ognl:3.3.5; label=fails-java-run; body_chars=8015

risks: OGNL 3.3.5 depends on `org.javassist:javassist:3.29.0-GA`, but it is a normal transitive runtime dependency of `ognl:ognl:3.3.5`, so adding it separately should not be necessary.
SecurityManager coverage is version-sensitive on JDK 25 and later; tests that require installing a SecurityManager instead of detecting unsupported installation can fail during Java run.

Javassist expression-compilation coverage may be harder under native image and should be guarded separately from basic expression evaluation if local native verification shows unsupported dynamic class generation.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: OGNL is a pure in-process expression/reflection library; no extra Maven dependency or Docker service is needed, but meaningful coverage on modern JDKs should run tests with module opens because OGNL and its own upstream JDK17+ test profile rely on reflective access to JDK packages.
